# Spec — 2026-05-16

## Executive Summary

- **anthropics/claude-code** (v2.1.143): Plugin dependency enforcement (`plugin disable` blocks when others depend on it); `worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` for repos where worktrees are impractical; stop hook block cap (turn ends after 8 consecutive blocks, configurable via `CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP`); `/bg` and `←`-detach now preserve `--fallback-model`, `--mcp-config`, `--settings`, `--add-dir`, `--plugin-dir`, `--strict-mcp-config`; PowerShell tool enabled by default on Windows for Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry
- **anthropics/claude-code** (v2.1.143): Stop hook infinite loop guard — hooks that repeatedly block are capped at 8 iterations with a configurable `CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP`. Our hooks have no loop/retry guard
- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (~120 commits): Semantic search tool improvements (only hint when enabled); session transcript export as markdown; `{file:...}` syntax for agent markdown prompts; configurable auto compaction threshold; voice input to inline review comments; Agent Manager worktree session continuation; PipeWire speech input support
- **cline/cline** (CLI v3.0.3): SAP AI Core Hosted LLMs support (non-orchestration direct model calls); macOS screenshot U+202F path resolution; `cline-sdk` skill for Claude Code agents; CI path filtering for workflows; typed model metadata facts refactoring
- **aider-ai/aider**: Bash tree-sitter tags for repomap support (single commit)
- **continuedev/continue**: No new commits in last 7 days
- **Key new pattern**: Stop hook block cap — Claude Code v2.1.143 stops looping after 8 consecutive hook blocks and ends the turn with a warning. Prevents runaway hook-tool interaction loops. Our hooks have no such guard
- **Key new pattern**: Plugin dependency graph enforcement — `plugin disable` refuses when another enabled plugin depends on the target, `plugin enable` force-enables transitive dependencies. Our plugin system has `dependencies` array check at load time but no disable/enable dependency cascade
- **Gap persists (from yesterday)**: Hook `continueOnBlock` rejection feedback — still unimplemented in our codebase
- **Gap persists (from yesterday)**: Chunked compaction for oversized payloads — still unimplemented (our `compactConversation` sends entire payload in one call)
- **Gap resolved (from yesterday)**: MCP Project Directory — already implemented at `src/mcp/client.ts:136`; Agent Observability Headers — already implemented at `src/providers/sessionHeaders.ts`

## What to build (prioritized)

### 1. Hook Rejection Feedback Loop (continueOnBlock)
- **What**: Add `continueOnBlock` option to PostToolUse hooks — when a hook rejects tool execution (non-zero exit / HTTP error), feed the rejection reason back to the model as an error message so it self-corrects instead of halting
- **Why**: Claude Code has stabilized this pattern across v2.1.139-143. Our hooks (`src/hooks/index.ts`) execute and return `HookResult[]` but the orchestrator doesn't feed rejection back into the conversation. This is the most impactful guardrail pattern for production autonomous agents — without it, hook rejections silently terminate the agent turn
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 `continueOnBlock`; stabilized in v2.1.140-143
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 2. Chunked Compaction for Oversized Payloads
- **What**: When messages to be compacted exceed the summarization model's input limit, split into chunks and summarize incrementally. Add `maxChunkTokens` to `CompactionOptions` in `src/core/compaction.ts`
- **Why**: Kilo shipped `fix(cli): chunk oversized compactions` (c1a797c1). Our `compactConversation()` at `src/core/compaction.ts:189` sends the entire `messagesToSummarize` block to the LLM in one call. For very long sessions (100k+ tokens of history), this exceeds model context and fails. Chunked compaction ensures summarization always succeeds regardless of session length
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode commit c1a797c1 "fix(cli): chunk oversized compactions"
- **Category**: core
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 3. Stop Hook Block Cap (Loop Guard)
- **What**: Add a maximum consecutive block count for Stop hooks. When a Stop hook blocks the same tool N times consecutively (default 8), end the turn with a warning instead of looping forever. Make configurable via `ALEXI_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP` env var
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.143 fixed "stop hooks that block repeatedly looping forever". Our `HookManager` has no loop detection — a misconfigured Stop hook that always rejects can cause infinite retries, locking the session. Single guard check in the orchestrator loop, minimal implementation effort
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.143 "Fixed stop hooks that block repeatedly looping forever — the turn now ends with a warning after 8 consecutive blocks"
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 4. Hook Event-Type Compatibility Validation
- **What**: Add validation that `SessionStart`, `SessionEnd`, and `Error` events only accept `command`-type hooks (not `http` or `script` hooks that may have long timeouts or rely on session state not yet available). Show a clear error at registration time
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.142 added "use a command-type hook instead" errors for incompatible event/type combinations. Our `HookDefinitionSchema` (line 76 in `src/hooks/index.ts`) validates structure but not semantic compatibility — an http hook on `SessionStart` could deadlock session startup waiting for a timeout. Prevents footgun misconfiguration
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.142 — "configuring a prompt- or agent-type hook for SessionStart/Setup/SubagentStart now shows a clear error"
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 5. File Content Inclusion in Agent Prompts ({file:...} syntax)
- **What**: Support `{file:path/to/file}` syntax in agent markdown prompt files that injects the referenced file content at that location, enabling composable agent prompts without duplication
- **Why**: Kilo-Org/kilocode shipped `feat(config): add {file:...} syntax support for agent markdown prompts` (commit edf91229/80edeee5). Our agent system in `src/agent/` loads markdown prompts but has no file inclusion mechanism — agent authors must duplicate shared context across multiple agent files. This is a developer ergonomics improvement for multi-agent setups
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode commit 80edeee5 "feat(config): add {file:...} syntax support for agent markdown prompts"
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: small (< 4h)
